revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''春祭り'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]に行われる[[祭り]]の総称。 2月の[[祈年祭]]を春祭り、11月の[[新嘗祭]]を[[秋祭り]]...'
revision.text:
'''春祭り'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]に行われる[[祭り]]の総称。
2月の[[祈年祭]]を春祭り、11月の[[新嘗祭]]を[[秋祭り]]とする場合もある。

'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]に行われる[[祭り]]の総称。
2月の[[祈年祭]]を春祭り、11月の[[新嘗祭]]を[[秋祭り]]とする場合もある。

'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]に行われる[[祭|祭り]]の総称。
2月の[[祈年祭]]を春祭り、11月の[[新嘗祭]]を[[秋祭り]]とする場合もある。

== 関連項目 == *[[夏祭り]] *[[太鼓の達人]] 太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭りというソフトがある。 *[[秋祭り]] *[[冬祭り]] *[[祈年祭]] *[[復活祭]] - イースター *[[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] *[[過越]] *[[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 *[[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ

{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はるまつり}} [[Category:祭]] [[Category:春]]

{{字引|date=2013年2月}} '''春祭り'''（はるまつり）とは[[春]]に行われる[[祭|祭り]]の総称である。
== 関連項目 == *[[夏祭り]] *[[太鼓の達人]] 太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭りというソフトがある。 *[[秋祭り]] *[[冬祭り]] *[[祈年祭]] *[[復活祭]] - イースター *[[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] *[[過越]] *[[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 *[[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ *[[例祭]] *[[桜祭り]] *[[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]]

{{字引|date=2013年2月}} '''春祭り'''（はるまつり）とは[[春]]に行われる[[祭|祭り]]の総称である。
== 関連項目 == *[[夏祭り]] *[[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある *[[秋祭り]] *[[冬祭り]] *[[祈年祭]] *[[復活祭]] - イースター *[[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] *[[過越]] *[[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 *[[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ *[[例祭]] *[[桜祭り]] *[[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]]
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はるまつり}} [[Category:祭]] [[Category:春]]

{{字引|date=2013年2月}} [[ファイル:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|[[葵祭]]（[[賀茂御祖神社]]および[[賀茂別雷神社]]の[[例祭]]）]] [[ファイル:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]例祭「春の山王祭」（[[高山祭]]の一つ）]] [[ファイル:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|[[日高神社]]例大祭（[[日高火防祭り|日高火防祭]]はその一部）]] [[ファイル:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[大國魂神社]]例大祭（[[くらやみ祭]]はその一部）]]
'''春祭'''<ref name="大辞泉">『[[大辞泉]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[マイペディア]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版</ref><ref>小学館『[[日本大百科全書]]：ニッポニカ』</ref>/'''春祭り'''<ref name="大辞泉" />（はるまつり）とは、[[春]]に行われる[[祭]]の総称である。
== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]の季語）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web |date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。 * 例句： <ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句： <ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句： 谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句： [[水槽]]に 貝の活けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" />
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はるまつり}} [[Category:祭]] [[Category:春]]

{{字引|date=2013年2月}} [[ファイル:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|[[葵祭]]（[[賀茂御祖神社]]および[[賀茂別雷神社]]の[[例祭]]）]] [[ファイル:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]例祭「春の山王祭」（[[高山祭]]の一つ）]] [[ファイル:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|[[日高神社]]例大祭（[[日高火防祭り|日高火防祭]]はその一部）]] [[ファイル:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[大國魂神社]]例大祭（[[くらやみ祭]]はその一部）]]
'''春祭'''<ref name="大辞泉">『[[大辞泉]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[マイペディア]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版</ref><ref>小学館『[[日本大百科全書]]：ニッポニカ』</ref>/'''春祭り'''<ref name="大辞泉" />（はるまつり）とは、[[春]]に行われる[[祭]]の総称である。
== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]もしくは三春の季語。[[歳時記]]によって異なる）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web |date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。 * 例句： <ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句： <ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句： 谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句： [[水槽]]に 貝の活けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" /> 子季語は無い。また、関連季語として挙げられているものは無いが、[[祈年祭]]を始め、数多くの春の祭が季語となっており、それらは現実として関連してはいる。
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はるまつり}} [[Category:祭]] [[Category:春]] [[Category:春の季語]]

'''春祭'''<ref name="大辞泉">『[[大辞泉]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[マイペディア]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版</ref><ref>小学館『[[日本大百科全書]]：ニッポニカ』</ref>/'''春祭り'''<ref name="大辞泉" />（はるまつり）とは、[[春]]に行われる[[祭]]の総称である。
== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]もしくは三春の季語。[[歳時記]]によって異なる）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web |date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。 * 例句： <ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句： <ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句： 谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句： [[水槽]]に 貝の活けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" /> 子季語は無い。また、関連季語として挙げられているものは無いが、[[祈年祭]]を始め、数多くの春の祭が季語となっており、それらは現実として関連してはいる。
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。

revision.comment: + / ※例句の表示にquotationテンプレを使用 / etc.
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|[[葵祭]]（[[賀茂御祖神社]]および[[賀茂別雷神社]]の[[例祭]]）]] [[ファイル:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]例祭「春の山王祭」（[[高山祭]]の一つ）]] [[ファイル:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|[[日高神社]]例大祭（[[日高火防祭り|日高火防祭]]はその一部）]] [[ファイル:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[大國魂神社]]例大祭（[[くらやみ祭]]はその一部）]]
'''春祭'''<ref name="大辞泉">『[[大辞泉]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[マイペディア]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版</ref><ref>小学館『[[日本大百科全書]]：ニッポニカ』</ref>/'''春祭り'''<ref name="大辞泉" />（はるまつり）とは、[[春]]に行われる[[祭]]の総称である。
== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]もしくは三春の季語。[[歳時記]]によって異なる）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web |date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。
なお、「'''春祭り'''」「'''春まつり'''」「'''はるまつり'''」などという表現は歳時記に存在せず、季語を厳格に限定しようとする論者の主張するところでは、[[仮名交じり]]の「春祭り」は季語ではなく、「春まつり」や仮名書きの「はるまつり」などは論外である。しかしながら、おおかたの専門家は自由な表現を重視しており、各人・各団体で許容範囲の差こそあれ、厳格すぎる縛りは避けている。
{{quotation| * 例句：<ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句：谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>[[水槽]]</rb><rt>すいさう</rt></ruby>に <ruby><rb>[[貝]]</rb><rt>かひ</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>活</rb><rt>い</rt></ruby>けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} 子季語は無い。また、関連季語として挙げられているものは無いが、[[祈年祭]]を始め、数多くの春の祭が季語となっており、それらは現実として関連してはいる。
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はるまつり}} [[Category:祭]] [[Category:春]] [[Category:春の季語]]

'''春祭'''<ref name="大辞泉">『[[大辞泉]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[マイペディア]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版</ref><ref>小学館『[[日本大百科全書]]：ニッポニカ』</ref>/'''春祭り'''<ref name="大辞泉" />（はるまつり）とは、[[春]]に行われる[[祭]]の総称である。
== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]もしくは三春の季語〈[[歳時記]]によって異なる〉）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web |date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。 {{quotation| * 例句：<ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句：谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>[[水槽]]</rb><rt>すいさう</rt></ruby>に <ruby><rb>[[貝]]</rb><rt>かひ</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>活</rb><rt>い</rt></ruby>けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} 子季語は無い。また、関連季語として挙げられているものは無いが、[[祈年祭]]を始め、数多くの春の祭が季語となっており、それらは現実として関連してはいる。
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。

== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]もしくは三春の季語〈[[歳時記]]によって異なる〉）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。 {{quotation| * 例句：<ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句：谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>[[水槽]]</rb><rt>すいさう</rt></ruby>に <ruby><rb>[[貝]]</rb><rt>かひ</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>活</rb><rt>い</rt></ruby>けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} 子季語は無い。また、関連季語として挙げられているものは無いが、[[祈年祭]]を始め、数多くの春の祭が季語となっており、それらは現実として関連してはいる。
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * {{ill2|マルテニツァ|en|Martenitsa}}（マルツィショール、マルティンキ） - 東欧の春祭り。2017年の[[国際連合教育科学文化機関]]（ユネスコ）[[無形文化遺産の一覧|無形文化遺産]]登録 * {{ill2|フドゥレルレス|en|Hıdırellez}} - トルコ・マケドニアの春の祭り。2017年の無形文化遺産登録。 * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[ウマウアケーニョ]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はるまつり}} [[Category:祭]] [[Category:春]] [[Category:春の季語]]

'''春祭'''<ref name="大辞泉">『[[大辞泉]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[マイペディア]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版</ref><ref>小学館『[[日本大百科全書]]：ニッポニカ』</ref>/'''春祭り'''<ref name="大辞泉" />（はるまつり）とは、[[春]]に行われる[[祭]]の総称である。
== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]もしくは三春の季語〈[[歳時記]]によって異なる〉）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。 {{quotation| * 例句：<ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句：谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>[[水槽]]</rb><rt>すいさう</rt></ruby>に <ruby><rb>[[貝]]</rb><rt>かひ</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>活</rb><rt>い</rt></ruby>けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} 子季語は無い。また、関連季語として挙げられているものは無いが、[[祈年祭]]を始め、数多くの春の祭が季語となっており、それらは現実として関連してはいる。
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * {{ill2|マルテニツァ|en|Martenitsa}}（マルツィショール、マルティンキ） - 東欧の春祭り。2017年の[[国際連合教育科学文化機関]]（ユネスコ）[[無形文化遺産の一覧|無形文化遺産]]登録 * {{ill2|フドゥレルレス|en|Hıdırellez}} - トルコ・マケドニアの春の祭り。2017年の無形文化遺産登録。 * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[花祭り (フォルクローレ)|ウマウアケーニョ（花祭り）]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Otheruses}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}} {{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り}} held in {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦}}'s seventh month are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon D..."

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』
{{Event-stub}} [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Spring]] [[Category:Spring seasonal words]]

While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

== Related Topics == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
* '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

* '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! revision.text:
[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
* '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』
{{Event-stub}} [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Spring]] [[Category:Spring seasonal words]]

* '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! revision.text:
[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
* '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』
{{Event-stub}} [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Spring]] [[Category:Spring seasonal words]]

* '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
* '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

revision.text:
[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

revision.text:
[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』
{{Event-stub}} [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Spring]] [[Category:Spring seasonal words]]

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

revision.text:
[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
