[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』
{{Event-stub}} [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Spring]] [[Category:Spring seasonal words]]

'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

revision.text:
[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語|12=draft}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=draft}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記|12=draft}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=draft}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子|12=draft}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷|12=draft}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=draft}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典|12=draft}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書|12=draft}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭|12=draft}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春|12=draft}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語|12=draft}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=draft}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記|12=draft}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=draft}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子|12=draft}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷|12=draft}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=draft}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.

== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語|12=draft}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=draft}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記|12=draft}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=draft}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子|12=draft}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷|12=draft}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=draft}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典|12=draft}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書|12=draft}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭|12=draft}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春|12=draft}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語|12=draft}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=draft}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記|12=draft}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=draft}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子|12=draft}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷|12=draft}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=draft}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=draft}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=draft}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭|12=draft}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=draft}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa|12=draft}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=draft}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜|12=draft}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越|12=draft}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典|12=draft}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)|12=draft}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人|12=draft}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|12=draft}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}})]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=draft}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭|12=draft}})]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hidaka Shrine|ja|日高神社|12=draft}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hidaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り|12=draft}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Okunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=draft}})]]
'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典|12=draft}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書|12=draft}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭|12=draft}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春|12=draft}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語|12=draft}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=draft}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記|12=draft}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=draft}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子|12=draft}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷|12=draft}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=draft}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=draft}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=draft}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭|12=draft}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=draft}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa|12=draft}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=draft}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜|12=draft}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越|12=draft}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典|12=draft}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)|12=draft}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人|12=draft}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

'''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=draft}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|ja|世界大百科事典|12=draft}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Nipponica|ja|日本大百科全書|12=draft}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|festivals|ja|祭|12=draft}} held in the {{ill|spring|ja|春|12=draft}}.
== Seasonal Word == As a {{ill|kigo|ja|季語|12=draft}}, the term '''Haru Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=draft}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|saijiki|ja|歳時記|12=draft}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=draft}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Haru Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūson Mizuhara|ja|水原秋桜子|12=draft}}<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Haru Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Hakyō Ishida|ja|石田波郷|12=draft}} * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Haru Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=draft}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Haru Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Haru Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=draft}} ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=draft}} ─ {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|ja|冬祭|12=draft}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Haru Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Natsu Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=draft}}) ─ {{ill|Aki Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Easter|ja|復活祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa|12=draft}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|ja|無形文化遺産の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=draft}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * {{ill|Walpurgis Night|ja|ヴァルプルギスの夜|12=draft}} * {{ill|Passover|ja|過越|12=draft}} * {{ill|The Rite of Spring|ja|春の祭典|12=draft}} – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)|12=draft}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cherry blossom festival|ja|桜祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taiko no Tatsujin|ja|太鼓の達人|12=draft}} – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Haru Matsuri』

[[ファイル:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|[[葵祭]]（[[賀茂御祖神社]]および[[賀茂別雷神社]]の[[例祭]]）]] [[ファイル:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]例祭「春の山王祭」（[[高山祭]]の一つ）]] [[ファイル:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|[[日高神社]]例大祭（[[日高火防祭り|日高火防祭]]はその一部）]] [[ファイル:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[大國魂神社]]例大祭（[[くらやみ祭]]はその一部）]]
'''春祭'''<ref name="大辞泉">『[[大辞泉]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[マイペディア]]』</ref><ref>平凡社『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版</ref><ref>小学館『[[日本大百科全書]]：ニッポニカ』</ref>/'''春祭り'''<ref name="大辞泉" />（はるまつり）とは、[[春]]に行われる[[祭]]の総称である。
== 季語 == [[季語]]としての'''春祭'''（はるまつり）は、[[春]]の季語（[[初春]]もしくは三春の季語〈[[歳時記]]によって異なる〉）である<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=https://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=季語と歳時記－きごさい歳時記 |publisher=季語と歳時記の会 |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref>。分類は[[行事]]/人事<ref>「行事」も「人事」も、ここでは、人間が行う事柄を指す。</ref>。季語においては、春季に行われる祭全般を指す<ref name="Kigosai" />。 {{quotation| * 例句：<ruby><rb>老桑</rb><rt>らうさう</rt></ruby>の <ruby><rb>瑞</rb><rt>ずい</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>芽立</rb><rt>めだち</rt></ruby>や '''春祭''' ─ [[水原秋桜子]] <ref name="大辞泉" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>山</rb><rt>やま</rt></ruby><ruby><rb>下</rb><rt>お</rt></ruby>りて [[もんぺ]]<ruby><rb>鮮</rb><rt>すくな</rt></ruby>し '''春祭''' ─ [[石田波郷]] * 例句：谷々に [[乗鞍岳|乗鞍]]見えて '''春祭''' ─ [[前田普羅]] 『飛騨紬』（1947年〈昭和22年〉刊）<ref name="Kigosai" /> * 例句：<ruby><rb>[[水槽]]</rb><rt>すいさう</rt></ruby>に <ruby><rb>[[貝]]</rb><rt>かひ</rt></ruby>の<ruby><rb>活</rb><rt>い</rt></ruby>けある '''春祭''' ─ 広江八重楼 『筑摩文学全集』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} 子季語は無い。また、関連季語として挙げられているものは無いが、[[祈年祭]]を始め、数多くの春の祭が季語となっており、それらは現実として関連してはいる。
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * '''春祭''' ─ [[夏祭]] ─ [[秋祭]] ─ [[冬祭]] * 季語としての祭 ： '''春祭''' ─ 祭（[[夏祭]]） ─ [[秋祭]] * [[祈年祭]] * [[復活祭]] - イースター * {{ill2|マルテニツァ|en|Martenitsa}}（マルツィショール、マルティンキ） - 東欧の春祭り。2017年の[[国際連合教育科学文化機関]]（ユネスコ）[[無形文化遺産の一覧|無形文化遺産]]登録 * {{ill2|フドゥレルレス|en|Hıdırellez}} - トルコ・マケドニアの春の祭り。2017年の無形文化遺産登録。 * [[ヴァルプルギスの夜]] * [[過越]] * [[春の祭典]] - イーゴリ・ストラヴィンスキーのバレエ音楽 * [[花祭り (フォルクローレ)|ウマウアケーニョ（花祭り）]] - アンデスのフォルクローレ * [[例祭]] * [[桜祭り]] * [[丸山公園 (安芸高田市)]] * [[太鼓の達人]] - 『太鼓の達人 ドキッ!新曲だらけの春祭り』というソフトがある。
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はるまつり}} [[Category:祭]] [[Category:春]] [[Category:春の季語]]

[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|[[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]] (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine]])]]
[[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]])]]
[[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hitaka Shrine|ja|日高神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Shrine|qq=}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|qq=}})]]
[[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Okunitama Shrine]] Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}})]]

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|qq=|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|qq=|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|qq=|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|qq=|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

== Notes and References ==
{{Reflist}}

== See Also ==

* Festivals as seasonal words: '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Summer Matsuri|ja|夏祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏祭り}}) ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋祭り}}
* [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]]

* {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidrellez|qq=}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list
* [[:en:Walpurgis Night|Walpurgis Night]]

* {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower Festival (Folklore)|qq=|1=El Humahuaqueño|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11289071}} – Andean folklore
* {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}}

* [[:en:Taiko no Tatsujin|Taiko no Tatsujin]] – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Spring Matsuri』

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|qq=|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|qq=|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|qq=|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|qq=|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

== See Also == * '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Summer Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏祭り}} ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋祭り}} ─ {{ill|Winter Matsuri|ja|冬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬祭り}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Summer Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏祭り}}) ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋祭り}} * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * [[:en:Easter|Easter]] * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martenitsa|qq=}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the [[:en:UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists]] * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidrellez|qq=}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * [[:en:Walpurgis Night|Walpurgis Night]] * [[:en:Passover|Passover]] * [[:en:The Rite of Spring|The Rite of Spring]] – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower Festival (Folklore)|1=El Humahuaqueño|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11289071}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} * [[:en:Cherry Blossom Festival|Cherry blossom festival]] * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|1=Maruyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654729}} * [[:en:Taiko no Tatsujin|Taiko no Tatsujin]] – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Spring festivals in Japan]][[ja:春祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:12, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|[[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]] (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine]])]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]])]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hitaka Shrine|ja|日高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Shrine|qq=}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|qq=}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Okunitama Shrine]] Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}})]]
'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].
== Seasonal Word == As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]] * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Summer Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏祭り}} ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋祭り}} ─ {{ill|Winter Matsuri|ja|冬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬祭り}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Summer Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏祭り}}) ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋祭り}} * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * [[:en:Easter|Easter]] * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martenitsa|qq=}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the [[:en:UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists]] * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidrellez|qq=}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * [[:en:Walpurgis Night|Walpurgis Night]] * [[:en:Passover|Passover]] * [[:en:The Rite of Spring|The Rite of Spring]] – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower Festival (Folklore)|1=El Humahuaqueño|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11289071}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} * [[:en:Cherry Blossom Festival|Cherry blossom festival]] * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|1=Maruyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654729}} * [[:en:Taiko no Tatsujin|Taiko no Tatsujin]] – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Spring Matsuri』

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

* {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidrellez|qq=}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list

[[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]])]]
[[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hitaka Shrine|ja|日高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Shrine|qq=}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|qq=}})]]
[[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Okunitama Shrine]] Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}})]]

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].

== Seasonal Word ==
As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]]
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Spring festivals in Japan]][[ja:春祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Spring festivals in Japan]][[ja:春祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Spring festivals in Japan]][[ja:春祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:12, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|[[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]] (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Shrine]] and [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine]])]] [[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]])]] [[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hitaka Shrine|ja|日高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Shrine|qq=}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|qq=}})]] [[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|[[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Okunitama Shrine]] Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}})]]
'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『[[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]]』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『[[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Daihyakka Jiten]]』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『[[:en:Encyclopedia Nipponica|Nipponica]]』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for [[:en:Festival|festivals]] held in the [[:en:Spring (season)|spring]].
== Seasonal Word == As a [[:en:Kigo|kigo]], the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the [[:en:Saijiki|saijiki]]).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" /> {{quotation| * Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – [[:en:Shūōshi Mizuhara|Shūson Mizuhara]]<ref name="Daijisen" /> * Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – [[:en:Ishida Hakyō|Hakyō Ishida]] * Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" /> * Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" /> }} There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.
== Notes and References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ {{ill|Summer Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏祭り}} ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋祭り}} ─ {{ill|Winter Matsuri|ja|冬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬祭り}} * Festivals as seasonal words: '''Spring Matsuri''' ─ Festivals ({{ill|Summer Matsuri|ja|夏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏祭り}}) ─ {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋祭り}} * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] * [[:en:Easter|Easter]] * {{ill|Martenitsa|en|Martenitsa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Martenitsa|qq=}} (Martisor, Martinki) – A spring festival in Eastern Europe, inscribed in 2017 on the [[:en:UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists]] * {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidrellez|qq=}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list * [[:en:Walpurgis Night|Walpurgis Night]] * [[:en:Passover|Passover]] * [[:en:The Rite of Spring|The Rite of Spring]] – Ballet music by Igor Stravinsky * {{ill|Flower Festival (Folklore)|ja|花祭り (フォルクローレ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower Festival (Folklore)|1=El Humahuaqueño|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11289071}} – Andean folklore * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} * [[:en:Cherry Blossom Festival|Cherry blossom festival]] * {{ill|Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|ja|丸山公園 (安芸高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Park (Akitakata)|1=Maruyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654729}} * [[:en:Taiko no Tatsujin|Taiko no Tatsujin]] – Includes the software titled 『Taiko no Tatsujin Dokki! Shinkyoku Darake no Spring Matsuri』
