'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|en|Daijisen|WD=Q5209153|lt=Daijisen}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=Sekai Daihyakka Jiten}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Encyclopedia Nipponica|en|Encyclopedia Nipponica|WD=Q2660112|lt=Nipponica}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=festivals}} held in the {{ill|Spring (season)|en|Spring (season)|WD=Q1312|lt=spring}}.

== Seasonal Word ==
As a {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo}}, the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|Saijiki|en|Saijiki|WD=Q7400100|lt=saijiki}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūōshi Mizuhara|en|Shūōshi Mizuhara|WD=Q1940999|lt=Shūson Mizuhara}}<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Ishida Hakyō|en|Ishida Hakyō|WD=Q1673877|lt=Hakyō Ishida}}
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|en|Daijisen|WD=Q5209153|lt=Daijisen}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=Sekai Daihyakka Jiten}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Encyclopedia Nipponica|en|Encyclopedia Nipponica|WD=Q2660112|lt=Nipponica}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=festivals}} held in the {{ill|Spring (season)|en|Spring (season)|WD=Q1312|lt=spring}}.

== Seasonal Word ==
As a {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo}}, the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|Saijiki|en|Saijiki|WD=Q7400100|lt=saijiki}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūōshi Mizuhara|en|Shūōshi Mizuhara|WD=Q1940999|lt=Shūson Mizuhara}}<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Ishida Hakyō|en|Ishida Hakyō|WD=Q1673877|lt=Hakyō Ishida}}
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Spring festivals in Japan]][[ja:春祭り]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Spring festivals in Japan]][[ja:春祭り]]



[[File:Aoi Matsuri.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|WD=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri}} (the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Shrine}} and {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo-wakeikazuchi Shrine}})]]
[[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival}})]]
[[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hitaka Shrine|ja|日高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Shrine|qq=}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|qq=}})]]
[[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Okunitama Shrine}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}})]]

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|en|Daijisen|WD=Q5209153|lt=Daijisen}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=Sekai Daihyakka Jiten}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Encyclopedia Nipponica|en|Encyclopedia Nipponica|WD=Q2660112|lt=Nipponica}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=festivals}} held in the {{ill|Spring (season)|en|Spring (season)|WD=Q1312|lt=spring}}.

== Seasonal Word ==
As a {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo}}, the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|Saijiki|en|Saijiki|WD=Q7400100|lt=saijiki}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūōshi Mizuhara|en|Shūōshi Mizuhara|WD=Q1940999|lt=Shūson Mizuhara}}<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Ishida Hakyō|en|Ishida Hakyō|WD=Q1673877|lt=Hakyō Ishida}}
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

* {{ill|Hidrellez|en|Hıdırellez|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidrellez|qq=}} – A spring festival in Turkey and Macedonia, inscribed in 2017 on the Intangible Cultural Heritage list

== Seasonal Word ==
As a {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo}}, the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|Saijiki|en|Saijiki|WD=Q7400100|lt=saijiki}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūōshi Mizuhara|en|Shūōshi Mizuhara|WD=Q1940999|lt=Shūson Mizuhara}}<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Ishida Hakyō|en|Ishida Hakyō|WD=Q1673877|lt=Hakyō Ishida}}
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|en|Daijisen|WD=Q5209153|lt=Daijisen|1=Daijisen}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|1=Heibonsha World Encyclopedia}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Encyclopedia Nipponica|en|Encyclopedia Nipponica|WD=Q2660112|lt=Nipponica|1=Encyclopedia Nipponica}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=festivals|1=Festival}} held in the {{ill|Spring (season)|en|Spring (season)|WD=Q1312|lt=spring|1=Spring (season)}}.

== Seasonal Word ==
As a {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo|1=Kigo}}, the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|Saijiki|en|Saijiki|WD=Q7400100|lt=saijiki|1=Saijiki}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūōshi Mizuhara|en|Shūōshi Mizuhara|WD=Q1940999|lt=Shūson Mizuhara|1=Shūōshi Mizuhara}}<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Ishida Hakyō|en|Ishida Hakyō|WD=Q1673877|lt=Hakyō Ishida|1=Ishida Hakyō}}
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.

[[File:神楽台 (岐阜県高山市) - panoramio (2).jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}} Reisai "Haru no Sannō Matsuri" (part of {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama Festival|1=Takayama Festival}})]]
[[File:はやし屋台.JPG|thumb|230px|{{ill|Hitaka Shrine|ja|日高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Shrine|qq=}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Hitaka Hibuse Festival|ja|日高火防祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitaka Hibuse Festival|qq=}})]]
[[File:Okunitama-jinja-30.jpg|thumb|230px|{{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Okunitama Shrine|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} Reisai (part of {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}})]]

'''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭)<ref name="Daijisen">『{{ill|Daijisen|en|Daijisen|WD=Q5209153|lt=Daijisen|1=Daijisen}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|MyPedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339782}}』</ref><ref>Heibonsha 『{{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=Sekai Daihyakka Jiten|1=Heibonsha World Encyclopedia}}』, 2nd Edition</ref><ref>Shogakukan 『{{ill|Encyclopedia Nipponica|en|Encyclopedia Nipponica|WD=Q2660112|lt=Nipponica|1=Encyclopedia Nipponica}}』</ref>, also called '''Spring Festival''' or '''Haru no Matsuri''' (はるまつり), is a general term for {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=festivals|1=Festival}} held in the {{ill|Spring (season)|en|Spring (season)|WD=Q1312|lt=spring|1=Spring (season)}}.

== Seasonal Word ==
As a {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo|1=Kigo}}, the term '''Spring Matsuri''' (春祭り) represents a seasonal word for spring (either for {{ill|early spring|ja|初春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/early spring|1=Hatsuharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16870571}} or the three months of spring, depending on the {{ill|Saijiki|en|Saijiki|WD=Q7400100|lt=saijiki|1=Saijiki}}).<ref name="Kigosai">{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-02-16 |url=http://kigosai.sub.jp/kigo500e/341.html |title=春祭 |work=Kigosai Saijiki |publisher=Kigosai Saijiki Association |accessdate=2018-02-17}}</ref> It falls under the classification of {{ill|events|ja|行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/events|1=events|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625998}} or human affairs.<ref>"Events" and "human affairs" refer here to activities performed by humans.</ref> In terms of seasonal words, it encompasses festivals held during the spring season.<ref name="Kigosai" />
{{quotation|
* Example: The '''Spring Matsuri''' begins with the sprouting of old mulberries – {{ill|Shūōshi Mizuhara|en|Shūōshi Mizuhara|WD=Q1940999|lt=Shūson Mizuhara|1=Shūōshi Mizuhara}}<ref name="Daijisen" />
* Example: '''Spring Matsuri''' beneath the mountains, few fresh monpe in sight – {{ill|Ishida Hakyō|en|Ishida Hakyō|WD=Q1673877|lt=Hakyō Ishida|1=Ishida Hakyō}}
* Example: Valleys open to the sight of Norikura at '''Spring Matsuri''' – {{ill|Fura Maeda|ja|前田普羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fura Maeda|1=Fura Maeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397594}} 『Hida Tsumugi』 (published 1947)<ref name="Kigosai" />
* Example: Shellfish alive in a tank at '''Spring Matsuri''' – Yaehiro Hiroe 『Chikuma Literary Anthology』<ref name="Kigosai" />
}}
There are no sub-seasonal words for it. While no directly related seasonal words are listed, many spring festivals, such as the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, are seasonal words, which naturally relate to Spring Matsuri.
